scenarios.source: API health endpoint returns 200 on GET /health
API returns 200 on GET /api/tasks with a JSON array of tasks
API returns 201 on POST /api/tasks with JSON body {"title": "<title>", "description": "<optional>"}
API returns 200 on PATCH /api/tasks/{id} with JSON body {"completed": true} or {"completed": false}
API returns 204 on DELETE /api/tasks/{id}
API returns 200 on GET /api/tasks/{id} with the matching task JSON

ui.instruction: Open http://localhost:8080/tasks/ and verify the page title is "Sample App - Tasks"

scenarios.source: Tasks page loads at http://localhost:8080/tasks/ with page title "Sample App - Tasks"

ui.instruction: Open http://localhost:8080/tasks/ and verify the task list is visible

scenarios.source: User can open the Tasks page and see the task list

ui.instruction: On http://localhost:8080/tasks/, click "New Task", fill title and description, save the form

scenarios.source: User can click "New Task", fill title and description, and save

ui.instruction: After creating a task, verify its title appears in the task list on the Tasks page

scenarios.source: New task appears in the list with correct title

ui.instruction: On the Tasks page, click "Mark Done" on a task and verify a Done badge with strikethrough title

scenarios.source: User can click "Mark Done" on a task and see a Done badge with strikethrough title

ui.instruction: On the Tasks page, click "Mark Active" on a completed task and verify the done state clears

scenarios.source: User can click "Mark Active" to clear the done state

ui.instruction: Delete a task using "Delete", confirm with "Confirm Delete", and verify it is removed from the list

scenarios.source: User can click "Delete", confirm with "Confirm Delete" in the modal, and the task is removed from the list

ui.instruction: Open the delete modal, click "Cancel" or press Escape, and verify the task remains in the list

scenarios.source: User can click "Cancel" or press Escape to dismiss the delete modal without deleting

ui.instruction: Click "Load Demo Tasks", verify 15 tasks appear, and scroll the task list

scenarios.source: User can click "Load Demo Tasks" to seed 15 tasks and scroll the task list

ui.instruction: Open http://localhost:8080/settings/ and verify the page title is "Sample App - Settings"

scenarios.source: Settings page loads at http://localhost:8080/settings/ with page title "Sample App - Settings"

ui.instruction: On Settings, choose Light or Dark theme, click "Save Settings", and verify "Settings saved." appears

scenarios.source: User can choose Light or Dark theme and click "Save Settings" to see "Settings saved."

ui.instruction: On Settings, choose a default sort order (Newest first, Oldest first, or Title A-Z) and save

scenarios.source: User can choose a default sort order (Newest first, Oldest first, Title A-Z) on Settings

ui.instruction: On the Tasks page, type in the search box and verify the visible task list filters as you type

scenarios.source: Tasks page search box filters the visible list as the user types

ui.instruction: Use the Import Tasks wizard: paste titles, click Next, review preview, and Confirm Import

scenarios.source: User can click "Import Tasks", paste titles (one per line), click Next, review preview, and Confirm Import

ui.instruction: After confirming import in the wizard, verify imported tasks appear in the task list

scenarios.source: Imported tasks appear in the task list via POST /api/tasks

ui.instruction: On the Tasks page, click a task title and verify the browser opens the task detail URL /tasks/{id}

scenarios.source: User can click a task title on the Tasks page to open http://localhost:8080/tasks/{id}

ui.instruction: Open a task detail page and verify title "Sample App - Task Detail" with full title and description

scenarios.source: Task detail page shows page title "Sample App - Task Detail" with full title and description

ui.instruction: Open http://localhost:8080/ and verify the Task overview dashboard shows total, active, and done counts

scenarios.source: Home page at http://localhost:8080/ shows a Task overview with total, active, and done counts

ui.instruction: On the home page, verify recent task links navigate to task detail pages

scenarios.source: Recent tasks on the home page link to task detail pages

ui.instruction: Compare home dashboard total, active, and done counts against the task list from GET /api/tasks

scenarios.source: Dashboard counts match GET /api/tasks results

ui.instruction: Open http://localhost:8080/ and verify the page title is "Sample App"

scenarios.source: Frontend landing page loads at / with title "Sample App"
Unit tests pass via `pytest -q`
